message.content:
Interesting - the stopped process is actually `train_final.py`, not `quick_train.py`. The previous long-running script was still in the foreground. Let me kill it and start fresh.

{"analysis":"The stopped process is train_final.py (the old wordNgrams=3 config), not quick_train.py. The quick_train.py may not have started properly. Need to kill old process and restart cleanly.","plan":"1. Kill all Python processes. 2. Check if data files exist. 3. Run quick_train.py fresh with unbuffered output.","commands":[{"keystrokes":"kill -9 %1 %2 2>/dev/null; kill -9 $(ps aux 2>/dev/null | grep python | grep -v grep | awk '{print $2}') 2>/dev/null; echo 'DONE'\n","duration":2.0},{"keystrokes":"ls -lh /app/train_lower.txt /app/train_minimal.txt 2>/dev/null; echo '---'; ls -lh /app/model*.bin 2>/dev/null\n","duration":1.0}]}
